tool.name: transeq
tool.summary: Translate nucleotide sequences in forward reading frames

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `transeq` translates nucleotide sequence records in forward reading frames using the shared Epithema standard genetic code. The v1 surface supports frame `1`, `2`, `3`, or `all`, emits protein FASTA records deterministically, and ignores trailing partial codons.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one nucleotide input path plus optional `--frame <1|2|3|all>`. Protein-classified input is rejected clearly. Translation is strict with respect to unsupported codons and residues.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one protein FASTA record per translated frame in stable source order. Output identifiers are suffixed as `<accession>.frameN`, descriptions are annotated with `translated protein frame N`, and molecule kind becomes `protein`.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `transeq` invocation in view and compares the Epithema FASTA payload against a committed expected output. The governed comparison validates strict forward frame-1 translation for the committed coding fixture.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema transeq`. Current validation covers forward frame selection, strict translation, trailing partial-codon truncation, and stable FASTA emission from the committed coding fixture.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports forward frames only and the standard genetic code only. It does not search reverse-complement frames, and ambiguous unsupported codons fail clearly instead of being expanded.

artifacts.label: Forward translation nucleotide fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-sequence fixture used for deterministic forward translation validation.

examples.title: Translate a coding-sequence fixture in frame 1
examples.description: Translates the committed coding-sequence fixture in forward frame 1 and emits deterministic protein FASTA output.

expected_outputs.label: Frame-1 translated FASTA
expected_outputs.description: A stable FASTA rendering of the translated protein records for the committed coding fixture.
